name: NCT03430037: Alleviation by Fisetin of Frailty, Inflammation, and Related Measures in Older Women
summary: ClinicalTrials.gov registry record for a phase 2 randomized, placebo-controlled fisetin study in older women; the 2026-06-30 v2 API snapshot lists enrolling-by-invitation status and no posted results section.

provenance.note: Registry source created from the retained ClinicalTrials.gov v2 API snapshot.

name: NCT03675724: Alleviation by Fisetin of Frailty, Inflammation, and Related Measures in Older Adults
summary: ClinicalTrials.gov registry record for a phase 2 randomized, placebo-controlled fisetin study in older adults; the 2026-06-30 v2 API snapshot lists enrolling-by-invitation status and no posted results section.

provenance.note: Registry source created from the retained ClinicalTrials.gov v2 API snapshot.

name: Targeting Cellular Senescence With Senolytics to Improve Skeletal Health in Older Humans
summary: ClinicalTrials.gov registry record for the completed phase 2 randomized trial of senolytic therapy in postmenopausal women, with posted results for bone turnover, BMD, SASP, and adverse events.

provenance.locator: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 protocol/results modules for NCT04313634
provenance.note: Registry metadata import with posted results available.

name: Senolytic Therapy to Modulate the Progression of Alzheimer's Disease (SToMP-AD) Study
summary: ClinicalTrials.gov registry record for an active-not-recruiting phase 2 dasatinib plus quercetin trial in Alzheimer's disease, checked as trial-watch evidence with no posted results as of the 2026-03-31 registry update.

provenance.locator: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 protocol/results modules for NCT04685590
provenance.note: Registry metadata import; not result-bearing unless posted results are present.

name: Fisetin to Improve Vascular Function in Older Adults
summary: ClinicalTrials.gov registry record for an active-not-recruiting phase 1/2 fisetin trial in older adults, checked as trial-watch evidence with no posted results as of the 2025-11-07 registry update.

provenance.locator: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 protocol/results modules for NCT06133634
provenance.note: Registry metadata import; not result-bearing unless posted results are present.

name: The Achilles' heel of senescent cells: from transcriptome to senolytic drugs
summary: Foundational 2015 Aging Cell paper using senescent-cell pro-survival network analysis to identify senolytics, including dasatinib plus quercetin, and reporting reduced senescent-cell burden plus functional improvements in aged, irradiated, and progeroid mouse contexts.

name: Senolytics improve physical function and increase lifespan in old age
short_name: D+Q old mouse healthspan and lifespan
summary: 2018 Nature Medicine paper reporting that intermittent dasatinib plus quercetin reduced senescent-cell burden, improved physical function, and increased post-treatment survival in aged mice.

name: Senolytics decrease senescent cells in humans: Preliminary report from a clinical trial of dasatinib plus quercetin in individuals with diabetic kidney disease
summary: 2019 EBioMedicine preliminary human trial report that tested intermittent dasatinib plus quercetin in diabetic kidney disease and measured senescent-cell and SASP-related biomarkers.

name: Senolytic drugs: from discovery to translation
summary: 2020 review summarizing senolytic discovery, candidate compounds, translational rationale, and human-trial boundary conditions.

name: Senolytics dasatinib and quercetin in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis: Results of a phase I, single-blind, single-center, randomized, placebo-controlled pilot trial on feasibility and tolerability
summary: 2023 EBioMedicine randomized pilot trial testing feasibility and tolerability of intermittent dasatinib plus quercetin in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.

name: Effects of intermittent senolytic therapy on bone metabolism in postmenopausal women: a phase 2 randomized controlled trial
summary: 2024 Nature Medicine phase 2 randomized controlled trial of intermittent dasatinib plus quercetin in postmenopausal women, focused on bone metabolism outcomes.

name: A pilot study of senolytics to improve cognition and mobility in older adults at risk for Alzheimer's disease
short_name: D+Q cognition and mobility AD-risk pilot
summary: 2025 EBioMedicine pilot study of intermittent dasatinib plus quercetin in older adults at risk for Alzheimer's disease, focused on feasibility, safety, cognition, mobility, and biomarkers.
